action.hypothesis: Because the frontier is empty, the next highest-value move is to establish a trustworthy K=128 quality anchor with the simplest mainline architecture before rewarding smaller K or drawing conclusions about new families. Run a proxy topk baseline at K=128 with the default expansion and optimizer explicitly pinned so later rounds can measure real FVU gains against a stable reference.
action.summary: No reliable K=128 anchor exists in memory yet. This round should spend one proxy run on an explicitly pinned mainline topk baseline so subsequent architecture work can target material FVU improvement rather than comparing against an implicit or drifting default.
action.self_review: This is intentionally conservative, but the memory is empty and the primary objective depends on having a trustworthy K=128 reference. I avoided architecture edits because there is not yet enough evidence to justify a new family over first establishing the anchor.
notes_to_memory: Round 0 starts with empty frontier and no trustworthy K=128 anchor, so anchor establishment takes priority over lower-K comparisons.
Treat this run as the baseline reference point for future aggressive FVU-improvement bets.
next_hypotheses: If this anchor is stable, escalate immediately to a higher-capacity or more expressive encoder family that could materially beat its FVU at the same K=128.
If the anchor quality is already much better than expected, then test quality-first upgrades such as mixture/factorized/residual encoders before considering smaller K.
